task_completion.description: Verify that the dental CBCT scan is loaded and visible in the software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blue Sky Plan interface should display the CBCT scan, typically showing axial, coronal, and sagittal slice views of a patient's head/jaw.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 3D surface model (mesh) has been generated from the scan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D mesh/model of the bony anatomy is visible in the 3D viewport of the software.

task_completion.description: Verify that the maxilla (upper jaw) and skull base have been removed from the 3D mesh.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D model in the viewport shows ONLY the lower jaw (mandible). The upper teeth, maxilla, and upper skull structures must be completely absent from the mesh.

task_completion.description: Verify that the mandible mesh is clean of noise and artifacts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The isolated mandible mesh appears smooth and continuous without floating disconnected pieces, scatter noise, or jagged artifact remnants around the bone.

task_completion.description: Verify the export settings are configured for the correct file path and format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or save window is visible showing the STL format selected and the destination path set to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\MandibleOnly.stl.

task_completion.description: Verify the final STL file was successfully exported to the correct location with a valid file size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window or terminal shows 'MandibleOnly.stl' present in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' with a file size of at least 500 KB.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Blue Sky Plan GUI to generate and clean the mesh.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the software's segmentation, surface generation, and cutting/cleaning tools rather than running a script in the terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actively cleaned the mesh rather than loading a pre-made clean STL.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate screenshots should show the progression from a full skull/jaw mesh to an isolated mandible using the software's cutting or region-growing tools.

integrity.description: Verify the file was exported by the software and not faked via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file 'MandibleOnly.stl' should be created via the software's export dialog, not by using a 'touch' command or renaming an unrelated file in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is C:\Users\Docker\Documents\MandibleOnly.stl. The exported STL file must be at least 500 KB (which corresponds to approximately 10,000 triangles) to be considered a valid, high-resolution mandible mesh. The project file is StandardCase.bsp, which opens in Blue Sky Plan. A valid mandible mesh typically has a minimum height of 30.0 mm.
